LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF 2299 SACRAMENTO STREET CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED. THE COURT AWARDS PLAINTIFF $23,000 IN FEES AND $1,154.85 IN COSTS (TOTAL $24,154.85) AS REASONABLE FEES AND COSTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES OR ORDER FILED THIS DATE FOR FULL TEXT.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Nov-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Terrizzi of Plastiras & Terrizzi telephone number 415-472-8100 for Plaintiff.
Leo Portal Defendant in Propria Persona.

Ruling: The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF 2299 SACRAMENTO STREET CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' Motion To Enforce Settlement is granted. The court awards plaintiff $23,000 in fees and $1,154.85 in costs (total $24,154.85) as reasonable fees and costs. The court believes that the claimed fees are excessive and the partner and paralegal billing rates are high, especially since some of the paralegal work was more secretarial in nature. Chavez v. City of Los Angeles (2010) 47 Cal.4th 970 does not apply to this case because plaintiff filed this case as a limited jurisdiction action. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)
